title: rubus bifrons subsp. cuspidiferus
intro: Rubus bifrons subsp. cuspidiferus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a deciduous shrub native to western North America. It is a type of raspberry with edible fruit and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Rubus bifrons subsp. cuspidiferus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall well-being.
